**[EXTRACTED CYBERSECURITY AND DATA PROTECTION PROVISIONS --- CUSTOMER MASTER AGREEMENTS]{.underline}**

**Prepared by the Office of the General Counsel** **Vantage Industrial Technologies, Inc.**

FOR INTERNAL USE ONLY --- ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGED / ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT

**December 5, 2024**

**Prepared by:** Daniel Ito, Associate General Counsel, at the direction of Priya Raghavan, General Counsel & Corporate Secretary

**Purpose:** The following are verbatim extracts of cybersecurity incident notification, data protection, and related remedies provisions from three customer master agreements identified as containing time-sensitive notification obligations potentially triggered by the cybersecurity incident discovered on November 18, 2024. These three customers --- Harmon Defense Solutions, Crestfield Aerospace, and Nexagen Manufacturing --- are among Vantage Industrial Technologies, Inc.\'s top ten customers by revenue. These ten customers collectively represent approximately 38% of total revenue, or approximately \$710.6 million of \$1.87 billion in projected FY2024 revenue.

**[Section 1: Harmon Defense Solutions --- Master Supply Agreement]{.underline}**

**Contract:** Master Supply Agreement between Vantage Industrial Technologies, Inc. and Harmon Defense Solutions, dated March 14, 2022, as amended (the \"Harmon MSA\"). The following provisions are extracted from **Section 9 --- Information Security and Data Protection**, specifically **Sections 9.4 and 9.5**.

**Section 9.4 --- Security Incident Notification**

> **9.4 Security Incident Notification.**
>
> \(a\) For purposes of this Section 9, a \"Security Incident\" shall mean any unauthorized access to, acquisition of, disclosure of, or exfiltration of Harmon Data, whether intentional or inadvertent, by any person or entity, including but not limited to employees, agents, or subcontractors of Supplier, or any third party. \"Harmon Data\" is defined in Section 9.1 of this Agreement and includes any data provided by or on behalf of Harmon Defense Solutions, including but not limited to procurement records, contract pricing, technical specifications, payment information, and contact information of Harmon personnel.
>
> \(b\) Supplier shall notify Harmon\'s Chief Information Security Officer and designated procurement representative in writing within forty-eight (48) hours of Supplier\'s discovery of a Security Incident that affects or is reasonably believed to affect Harmon Data. Such notification shall include, at a minimum, the following information:
>
> \(i\) the date and time of discovery of the Security Incident;
>
> \(ii\) a description of the nature and scope of the Security Incident, including the attack vector, if known;
>
> \(iii\) the categories and approximate volume of Harmon Data affected or reasonably believed to have been affected;
>
> \(iv\) a description of the measures taken or proposed to contain and remediate the Security Incident and to prevent recurrence; and
>
> \(v\) the name, title, email address, and telephone number of a designated point of contact for ongoing communications regarding the Security Incident.
>
> \(c\) Supplier shall provide updated written reports every seventy-two (72) hours following the initial notification until the Security Incident has been fully remediated and Harmon Data has been secured. Each update shall describe any material changes to the information previously reported and any additional remediation measures undertaken.
>
> \(d\) Supplier shall cooperate fully with any investigation conducted by Harmon or its designated agents, including providing access to relevant logs, forensic reports, and affected systems within the timeframes reasonably requested by Harmon.

**Section 9.5 --- Remedies for Security Incidents**

> **9.5 Remedies for Security Incidents.**
>
> \(a\) *Liquidated Damages.* In the event Supplier fails to provide the notification required under Section 9.4 within the prescribed forty-eight (48) hour period, Supplier shall pay to Harmon liquidated damages in the amount of Five Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$500,000) per Security Incident. The parties acknowledge and agree that this amount represents a reasonable estimate of the damages that Harmon would sustain as a result of delayed notification, which damages would be difficult to calculate with precision. The payment of liquidated damages under this Section 9.5(a) shall not limit or preclude Harmon from seeking any other remedies available under this Agreement, at law, or in equity.
>
> \(b\) *Termination.* In addition to and without limiting any other remedies available at law or in equity, Harmon shall have the right to terminate this Agreement and any outstanding purchase orders upon thirty (30) days\' written notice if (a) Supplier experiences a Security Incident that materially compromises Harmon Data, or (b) Supplier fails to comply with the notification or cooperation obligations set forth in Sections 9.4 through 9.6 of this Agreement.
>
> \(c\) *Indemnification.* Supplier shall defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Harmon, its affiliates, and their respective officers, directors, employees, and agents from and against any and all losses, damages, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys\' fees) arising out of or relating to any Security Incident caused by or attributable to Supplier\'s acts, omissions, or failure to maintain the security measures required under this Section 9. This indemnification obligation shall include, without limitation, costs associated with regulatory investigations, third-party claims, notification expenses, and credit monitoring services.
>
> \(d\) *Survival.* The obligations set forth in this Section 9 shall survive expiration or termination of this Agreement for a period of three (3) years.

**[Section 2: Crestfield Aerospace --- General Terms and Conditions of Purchase]{.underline}**

**Contract:** General Terms and Conditions of Purchase between Crestfield Aerospace and Vantage Industrial Technologies, Inc., effective September 1, 2021, as amended by Amendment No. 2 dated January 15, 2024 (the \"Crestfield GTCP\"). The following provisions are extracted from **Section 12 --- Cybersecurity and Data Safeguards**, specifically **Sections 12.2 and 12.3**.

**Section 12.2 --- Incident Response and Notification**

> **12.2 Incident Response and Notification.**
>
> \(a\) For purposes of this Section 12, a \"Cybersecurity Event\" means any actual or reasonably suspected (a) unauthorized access to or acquisition of Crestfield Confidential Information or Crestfield Systems Data, (b) compromise of the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of any system or network used by Seller to process, store, or transmit Crestfield Confidential Information, or (c) ransomware attack, denial of service attack, or other malicious cyber activity affecting Seller\'s systems that process Crestfield Confidential Information.
>
> \(b\) Seller shall notify Crestfield\'s Supplier Security Office via encrypted electronic communication at the address specified in Exhibit C within forty-eight (48) hours of Seller\'s discovery or reasonable belief of a Cybersecurity Event affecting Crestfield Confidential Information. Such notification shall include, at a minimum:
>
> \(i\) the date and time of discovery of the Cybersecurity Event;
>
> \(ii\) a description of the nature and scope of the Cybersecurity Event;
>
> \(iii\) the categories and approximate volume of Crestfield Confidential Information or Crestfield Systems Data affected or reasonably believed to have been affected;
>
> \(iv\) a description of the measures taken or proposed to contain and remediate the Cybersecurity Event;
>
> \(v\) the name and contact information of a designated point of contact for ongoing communications; and
>
> \(vi\) whether law enforcement has been notified and, if so, the identity of the agency and contact information for the investigating officer.
>
> \(c\) Seller shall, at Seller\'s expense, engage a qualified independent third-party forensic investigator reasonably acceptable to Crestfield to investigate the Cybersecurity Event and shall provide Crestfield with a copy of the forensic investigator\'s findings within thirty (30) days of the conclusion of the investigation. Seller shall not unreasonably delay the commencement or completion of such investigation.

**Section 12.3 --- Remedies**

> **12.3 Remedies.**
>
> \(a\) *Termination and Payment Suspension.* In the event of a material Cybersecurity Event, or Seller\'s failure to comply with its notification obligations under Section 12.2, Crestfield may, at its sole discretion, (a) terminate this Agreement and any outstanding purchase orders immediately upon written notice, and/or (b) suspend all payments to Seller pending completion of the forensic investigation referenced in Section 12.2. Payments suspended under this Section 12.3(a) shall resume, less any amounts offset under Section 12.3(b) below, within thirty (30) days of Crestfield\'s receipt and review of the forensic investigation report.
>
> \(b\) *Indemnification.* Seller shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Crestfield and its affiliates from and against any claims, losses, damages, costs, liabilities, and expenses (including reasonable legal fees and costs of credit monitoring services provided to affected individuals) arising from or related to a Cybersecurity Event attributable to Seller\'s systems or personnel. This obligation shall apply regardless of whether Crestfield terminates this Agreement.
>
> \(c\) *Audit Right.* Following a Cybersecurity Event, Crestfield shall have the right, upon reasonable notice, to audit Seller\'s cybersecurity controls, incident response procedures, and remediation efforts, at Seller\'s expense, for a period of twelve (12) months following discovery of the Cybersecurity Event. Seller shall provide reasonable cooperation and access to its personnel, systems, documentation, and facilities in connection with any such audit.
>
> \(d\) *Insurance.* Seller shall maintain cybersecurity liability insurance with minimum coverage of Ten Million Dollars (\$10,000,000) per occurrence throughout the term of this Agreement and for a period of twelve (12) months following its termination or expiration. Seller shall provide Crestfield with a certificate of insurance upon request and shall notify Crestfield of any material reduction in coverage within ten (10) business days.

**[Section 3: Nexagen Manufacturing --- Vendor Information Security Addendum]{.underline}**

**Contract:** Vendor Information Security Addendum to the Master Services Agreement between Nexagen Manufacturing and Vantage Industrial Technologies, Inc., effective June 30, 2023 (the \"Nexagen VISA\"). This Addendum supplements and is incorporated into the Master Services Agreement dated April 12, 2020, as amended. The following provisions are extracted from **Section 7 --- Incident Management**, specifically **Sections 7.8 and 7.9**.

**Section 7.8 --- Notification of Security Breaches**

> **7.8 Notification of Security Breaches.**
>
> \(a\) For purposes of this Addendum, a \"Security Breach\" means any unauthorized access to, use of, or disclosure of Nexagen Data, or any breach of Vendor\'s obligations under this Addendum that results in the compromise of the confidentiality or integrity of Nexagen Data. For the avoidance of doubt, a ransomware or malware event that affects systems storing or processing Nexagen Data shall constitute a Security Breach regardless of whether Nexagen Data is confirmed to have been accessed or exfiltrated.
>
> \(b\) Vendor shall notify Nexagen\'s VP of Information Technology and VP of Supply Chain in writing within forty-eight (48) hours of Vendor\'s discovery of a confirmed or suspected Security Breach. Notice shall be provided via both email and telephone to the contacts listed in Schedule A of this Addendum. A confirmed or suspected Security Breach is deemed discovered as of the time any employee, officer, or agent of Vendor first knows or reasonably should know that a Security Breach has occurred or may have occurred.
>
> \(c\) Vendor shall provide a preliminary written incident report within five (5) business days of the initial notification, including the information reasonably available at that time regarding the nature, scope, and estimated impact of the Security Breach. Such preliminary report shall identify, to the extent known, the categories and volume of Nexagen Data affected, the systems involved, the measures taken to contain the Security Breach, and any law enforcement agencies that have been contacted.
>
> \(d\) A final written report shall be provided within sixty (60) days of the initial notification. The final report shall include the results of any forensic investigation, root cause analysis, a comprehensive description of all Nexagen Data affected, and a detailed remediation plan, including steps taken to prevent recurrence.

**Section 7.9 --- Consequences of Breach**

> **7.9 Consequences of Breach.**
>
> \(a\) *Termination.* In the event of a Security Breach, Nexagen may terminate this Addendum and the underlying Master Services Agreement upon fifteen (15) days\' written notice. If the Security Breach is attributable to Vendor\'s failure to comply with the security standards set forth in Sections 7.1 through 7.7 of this Addendum, termination shall be effective immediately upon notice, and Vendor shall not be entitled to any cure period.
>
> \(b\) *Indemnification.* Vendor shall indemnify and hold harmless Nexagen and its affiliates, officers, directors, and employees from and against all damages, losses, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys\' fees and the costs of regulatory compliance, notification, and credit monitoring) arising from a Security Breach attributable to Vendor\'s systems, personnel, or subcontractors. This indemnification obligation shall survive termination of this Addendum and the underlying Master Services Agreement.
>
> \(c\) *Future Business Exclusion.* Nexagen reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to exclude Vendor from future bid opportunities and request-for-proposal processes for a period of up to twenty-four (24) months following a Security Breach. Such exclusion shall not give rise to any claim by Vendor for lost profits, expectation damages, or other compensation.

**[Comparative Summary Table]{.underline}**

The following table summarizes the key notification and remedies provisions across the three extracted agreements for quick reference:

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Provision**                   **Harmon Defense Solutions (§ 9.4--9.5)**   **Crestfield Aerospace (§ 12.2--12.3)**                             **Nexagen Manufacturing (§ 7.8--7.9)**
  ------------------------------- ------------------------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Notification Deadline**       48 hours from discovery                     48 hours from discovery                                             48 hours from discovery or suspicion

  **Notification Trigger**        Security Incident affecting Harmon Data     Cybersecurity Event affecting Crestfield Confidential Information   Confirmed or suspected Security Breach

  **Liquidated Damages**          \$500,000 per incident                      None                                                                None

  **Termination Right**           30 days\' notice                            Immediate                                                           15 days (or immediate if security standards failure)

  **Indemnification**             Full defense and indemnity                  Full defense and indemnity + credit monitoring costs                Full indemnity + regulatory compliance costs

  **Payment Suspension**          Not specified                               Yes, pending investigation                                          Not specified

  **Audit Right**                 Not specified                               12 months post-incident, at Vantage\'s expense                      Not specified

  **Forensic Report Sharing**     Not specified                               Required within 30 days of investigation conclusion                 Preliminary report within 5 business days; final report within 60 days

  **Future Business Exclusion**   Not specified                               Not specified                                                       Up to 24 months

  **Insurance Requirement**       Not specified                               \$10M minimum per occurrence                                        Not specified
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[Preparer\'s Note]{.underline}**

**NOTE:** The above provisions are extracted verbatim from the referenced agreements. Defined terms not reproduced here --- including \"Harmon Data\" (Harmon MSA § 9.1), \"Crestfield Confidential Information\" and \"Crestfield Systems Data\" (Crestfield GTCP § 12.1), \"Nexagen Data\" (Nexagen VISA § 7.1), and related definitions in each agreement --- are broad and, based on preliminary review, encompass the categories of data identified in the Thorngate Forensic Solutions interim status report dated December 1, 2024 --- specifically, customer master records, procurement contact details, contract pricing, and banking information for ACH payments (including routing numbers and account numbers).

As of December 5, 2024, no notifications have been sent to Harmon Defense Solutions, Crestfield Aerospace, or Nexagen Manufacturing under any of the above provisions. The forty-eight (48) hour notification deadline under each agreement expired no later than November 20, 2024.

These three agreements were identified from a review of Vantage\'s top twenty customer contracts. Additional customer agreements are under review for similar cybersecurity notification and incident response provisions. Results of the full contract review are expected by December 10, 2024.

**Prepared by:**

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

Daniel Ito Associate General Counsel Vantage Industrial Technologies, Inc.

**Reviewed by:**

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

Priya Raghavan General Counsel & Corporate Secretary Vantage Industrial Technologies, Inc.

Date: December 5, 2024

PRIVILEGED & CONFIDENTIAL --- ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE / ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT This document was prepared at the direction of counsel in anticipation of litigation and for the purpose of providing legal advice. Do not distribute outside the Office of the General Counsel without prior authorization.

ected by December 20, 2024. An interim status report received from Thorngate =

. We are continuing to work with Thorngate to refine the scope and nature of =

kups. The remaining 58 endpoints require manual rebuild, which is estimated t=

**PRIVILEGED AND CONFIDENTIAL --- PREPARED AT THE DIRECTION OF COUNSEL**

**[CYBERSECURITY INCIDENT STATUS REPORT]{.underline}**

**[INCIDENT INC-2024-0047]{.underline}**

**PRIVILEGED AND CONFIDENTIAL --- PREPARED AT THE DIRECTION OF COUNSEL**

**INTERNAL USE ONLY --- DO NOT DISTRIBUTE**

**Prepared by:** Derek Fong, Chief Information Security Officer **Report Date:** December 5, 2024 **Reporting Period:** November 18, 2024 through December 5, 2024 (Day 17)

**Distribution:**

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Recipient**                       **Title**
  ----------------------------------- ---------------------------------------
  Priya Raghavan                      General Counsel & Corporate Secretary

  Lauren Hessler                      Chief Technology Officer

  Daniel Ito                          Associate General Counsel
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Company:** Vantage Industrial Technologies, Inc. 4200 Commerce Park Drive, Suite 800 Columbus, Ohio 43219

All incident documentation maintained in ServiceNow (Incident Ticket INC-2024-0047). This document has been prepared at the direction of counsel and is intended to be protected by the attorney-client privilege and the work product doctrine. Recipients should not forward, copy, or distribute this document without the express written consent of the General Counsel.

Right-click to update Table of Contents

**[Section 1: Executive Summary]{.underline}**

On November 18, 2024, at approximately 2:17 AM EST, the Vantage Industrial Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ: VTIQ) Security Operations Center (\"SOC\") detected a ransomware attack involving the LockStar 3.0 variant targeting the Company\'s enterprise IT environment. The attack vector has been identified as a compromised VPN credential belonging to a third-party maintenance contractor. The credential was used to gain initial access to the Vantage corporate network, after which the threat actor conducted lateral movement, staged data for exfiltration, and ultimately deployed ransomware payloads across a significant portion of the Company\'s networked endpoints.

The scope of the encryption event encompasses 347 of approximately 2,100 networked endpoints within the Vantage corporate IT environment. Critical enterprise resource planning (\"ERP\") modules were impacted, including the finance, procurement, and order management modules, resulting in disruptions to core business operations that have required manual workaround processes during the remediation period.

Prior to deployment of the ransomware payload, the threat actor exfiltrated approximately 83 GB of data to an external IP address. Forensic analysis conducted by Thorngate Forensic Solutions, Inc. indicates with high confidence that the exfiltrated data potentially affects approximately 4,200 corporate customer records, including banking information such as ACH routing numbers and account numbers, procurement contact personally identifiable information, and contract pricing data.

Operational technology (\"OT\") and SCADA systems supporting the Company\'s manufacturing operations were not affected by this incident. These systems operate on an air-gapped network architecture that is physically and logically separated from the corporate IT environment. This network segmentation served as an effective control in limiting the blast radius of the attack to the corporate IT domain.

The threat actor left a ransom demand of 45 Bitcoin, which at the November 18, 2024 exchange rate of approximately \$45,100 per Bitcoin, equates to approximately \$2,029,500. Vantage has not paid the ransom and does not intend to pay the ransom.

As of the date of this report, 289 of the 347 affected endpoints have been restored from backups. The remaining 58 endpoints require manual rebuild and are estimated to be fully restored by December 18, 2024. The total estimated financial impact of the incident to date is approximately \$4.5 million, encompassing incident response costs, business disruption costs, and customer notification preparation costs.

Thorngate Forensic Solutions, Inc. was retained on November 19, 2024, under an engagement letter providing for a 60-day investigation timeline. Preliminary findings are due December 20, 2024. Thorngate\'s December 1, 2024 interim status report confirmed the nature and scope of the exfiltrated data.

The FBI Internet Crime Complaint Center (\"IC3\") was contacted on November 20, 2024, to report the incident. As of the date of this report, no request from law enforcement to delay any public disclosure related to this incident has been received.

The current remediation status, financial impact assessment, external notification status, and open risk items are detailed in the sections that follow.

**[Section 2: Detailed Chronological Timeline]{.underline}**

The following chronological timeline documents all significant events from the initial detection of the incident through the current report date of December 5, 2024. All times are reported in Eastern Standard Time (\"EST\"). Responsible parties are identified where applicable.

**[November 18, 2024 (Day 0 --- Detection and Initial Response)]{.underline}**

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Time (EST)**          **Event**                                                                   **Responsible Party**
  ----------------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------- -----------------------
  \~1:30 AM -- 2:15 AM    Data exfiltration window (retroactively identified)                         Threat Actor

  \~2:17 AM               SOC detection of anomalous network activity                                 SOC / IT Security

  \~4:45 AM               Ransomware deployment across 347 endpoints completed                        Threat Actor

  4:50 AM                 Ransom note discovered                                                      IT Security

  5:00 AM                 Incident Response Team activated (Tier 3)                                   Derek Fong (CISO)

  5:15 AM                 Compromised VPN credential disabled; all third-party VPN access suspended   IT Security

  7:00 AM                 Network segmentation implemented for affected ERP segments                  IT Infrastructure

  11:00 AM                CTO Lauren Hessler verbally informed                                        Derek Fong (CISO)
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**2:17 AM EST --- SOC Detection.** At approximately 2:17 AM EST, the SOC detected anomalous network activity originating from a VPN session authenticated with a credential belonging to a third-party maintenance contractor. Automated alerts were triggered in the Company\'s SIEM platform based on behavioral anomaly detection rules, including unusually high outbound data transfer volumes and lateral movement patterns inconsistent with the contractor\'s normal access profile. The SOC analyst on duty escalated the alert immediately to the on-call IT Security team lead.

**1:30 AM -- 2:15 AM EST --- Data Exfiltration (Retroactively Identified).** Subsequent forensic analysis determined that approximately 83 GB of data was transferred from internal file servers and database repositories to an external IP address during the window between approximately 1:30 AM and 2:15 AM EST, prior to the deployment of the ransomware payload. The two-phase nature of the attack --- data staging and exfiltration first, followed by encryption --- indicates a deliberate and planned operation by the threat actor. The exfiltration was completed before the ransomware deployment triggered the alerts that led to detection.

**4:45 AM EST --- Ransomware Deployment.** By approximately 4:45 AM EST, the threat actor had deployed the LockStar 3.0 ransomware variant across 347 of approximately 2,100 networked endpoints in the Vantage corporate IT environment. The encrypted modules included the ERP finance module, the procurement module, and the order management module. These three modules collectively support the Company\'s core transaction processing, accounts payable and receivable functions, purchase order management, and customer order fulfillment workflows.

**4:50 AM EST --- Ransom Note.** A ransom note was discovered on the encrypted systems demanding payment of 45 Bitcoin within 72 hours. At the November 18, 2024 exchange rate of approximately \$45,100 per Bitcoin, the ransom demand equated to approximately \$2,029,500 (calculated as 45 × \$45,100 = \$2,029,500). The ransom note included a Tor-based communication portal and a unique identifier for the Vantage compromise.

**5:00 AM EST --- Incident Response Team Activation.** CISO Derek Fong activated the Incident Response Team (\"IRT\") under Tier 3 classification pursuant to the Company\'s Cybersecurity Incident Response Plan (\"CIRP\"). Tier 3 classification criteria were met based on two factors: (a) the compromise involved more than 100 endpoints, and (b) critical system availability was disrupted for a period exceeding 4 hours. The following IRT members were activated in accordance with the CIRP designation:

> • Incident Commander: Derek Fong, Chief Information Security Officer
>
> • IT Infrastructure Lead
>
> • IT Security Lead
>
> • Facilities Representative
>
> • HR Representative

IRT composition was consistent with CIRP Section 3.2 (Incident Response Team Roster). All activated members reported to the incident war room by 5:30 AM EST.

**5:15 AM EST --- Credential Disablement and VPN Suspension.** The compromised VPN credential was disabled, and all third-party VPN access was suspended pending a comprehensive review of third-party access controls and credential security.

**7:00 AM EST --- Network Segmentation.** Network segmentation measures were implemented to isolate the affected ERP network segments from the broader corporate network. This action was taken to prevent any further lateral movement and to protect unaffected systems during the initial containment phase.

**11:00 AM EST --- CTO Notification.** CISO Derek Fong verbally informed Chief Technology Officer Lauren Hessler of the incident, its scope, and the initial containment actions underway.

**[November 19, 2024 (Day 1 --- Forensics Engagement and Initial Containment)]{.underline}**

Thorngate Forensic Solutions, Inc. (\"Thorngate\") was retained under an engagement letter executed on November 19, 2024. The engagement provides for a 60-day investigation timeline, with preliminary findings due by December 20, 2024. Thorngate personnel arrived on-site and commenced forensic imaging of affected endpoints, network log preservation, and initial analysis of the attack vector and exfiltration pathway.

The backup restoration process for the 347 encrypted endpoints was initiated. IT Infrastructure began prioritizing the restoration of the ERP finance module as the highest-priority system for business continuity.

Associate General Counsel Daniel Ito learned of the incident on this date through informal communication from an IT colleague. General Counsel Priya Raghavan was traveling internationally at the time and was not informed of the incident on November 19, 2024.

**[November 20, 2024 (Day 2 --- General Counsel Informed and Law Enforcement Contact)]{.underline}**

**9:00 AM EST.** General Counsel Priya Raghavan learned of the incident upon returning from international travel. Upon being informed, Ms. Raghavan immediately contacted outside securities counsel at Hollister Marsh LLP, 700 K Street NW, Washington, D.C. 20001. The lead partner assigned to the matter is Jonathan Beckett. Hollister Marsh was engaged to provide securities law counsel regarding the Company\'s obligations in connection with the cybersecurity incident.

The FBI Internet Crime Complaint Center (\"IC3\") was contacted on this date to report the cybersecurity incident. The IC3 acknowledged receipt of the report and assigned a reference number. No further investigative actions by the FBI have been communicated to Vantage as of the date of this report.

**[November 21, 2024 (Day 3 --- Insurer Notification)]{.underline}**

Ridgeline Insurance Group was notified of the cyber event under Policy No. CYB-2024-08871 on November 21, 2024. Notification to Ridgeline Insurance Group was completed approximately 73 hours after initial detection of the incident. The policy requires notification within 72 hours of discovery of a cyber event. Ridgeline acknowledged receipt and reserved rights regarding the timeliness of notice but has not denied coverage at this time.

**[November 22--24, 2024 (Days 4--6 --- Ongoing Remediation)]{.underline}**

Endpoint restoration from backups continued during this period. Thorngate continued forensic analysis of the exfiltrated data content, including the identification and classification of data types present in the approximately 83 GB exfiltrated dataset. No significant new developments or escalation events occurred during this period.

**[November 25, 2024 (Day 7 --- CEO Briefing)]{.underline}**

Chief Executive Officer Marcus Ellsworth received an initial briefing on the scope, status, and financial impact of the cybersecurity incident. The briefing was conducted by CISO Derek Fong and CTO Lauren Hessler. The CEO was informed of the attack vector, the number of affected endpoints, the estimated financial impact, the engagement of Thorngate, and the contact with FBI IC3.

**[November 26--30, 2024 (Days 8--12 --- Continued Remediation)]{.underline}**

Endpoint restoration work continued throughout the period. November 28 was observed as the Thanksgiving holiday, and no remediation activity occurred on that date. As of November 30, 2024, approximately 240 of the 347 affected endpoints had been restored from backups. Thorngate forensic analysis remained ongoing during this period.

**[December 1, 2024 (Day 13 --- Thorngate Interim Report)]{.underline}**

Thorngate Forensic Solutions, Inc. delivered an interim status report to the Incident Response Team. The key finding of the interim report is as follows:

Thorngate reports with \"high confidence\" that the exfiltrated data includes customer banking information (ACH routing numbers and account numbers), procurement contact personally identifiable information (\"PII\"), and contract pricing data for approximately 4,200 corporate customer records. Affected customer records include data associated with several of the Company\'s largest commercial accounts, including but not limited to Harmon Defense Solutions, Crestfield Aerospace, and Nexagen Manufacturing. Thorngate\'s analysis of the exfiltrated dataset is ongoing, and additional affected customer records may be identified as the investigation continues.

The interim report confirmed the attack vector as the compromised third-party VPN credential and noted as a preliminary root cause finding that the credential was not protected by multi-factor authentication (\"MFA\"). Thorngate\'s full preliminary findings report remains on schedule for delivery by December 20, 2024.

**[December 3, 2024 (Day 15 --- Audit Committee Chair Notification)]{.underline}**

Audit Committee Chair Dr. Helen Ostrowski received an email summary from General Counsel Priya Raghavan providing an overview of the cybersecurity incident, its scope, the estimated financial impact, and the current remediation status. This communication represented the first Board-level notification regarding the incident. The next regularly scheduled meeting of the Audit Committee is January 22, 2025.

**[December 5, 2024 (Day 17 --- Current Date / This Report)]{.underline}**

This status report has been prepared and distributed to the recipients listed on the cover page. The current status of key workstreams and external communications is summarized below:

**Remediation Status.** As of the date of this report, 289 of 347 encrypted endpoints have been restored from backups. The remaining 58 endpoints require manual rebuild procedures and are estimated to be fully restored by December 18, 2024.

**Regulatory Status.** As of the date of this report, no Form 8-K or other public disclosure has been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission regarding this incident. No formal materiality determination has been conducted.

**Customer Notification Status.** Customer notifications regarding the potential exposure of data have not been issued as of this date. The Legal department is coordinating with Thorngate to finalize the scope of affected records before initiating notifications. No target date for customer notification has been established.

**Public Communications.** No press release or other public statement regarding this incident has been issued. No media inquiries have been received as of this report date.

**Law Enforcement.** FBI IC3 was contacted on November 20, 2024. As of this date, no request has been made by law enforcement to delay any public disclosure related to this incident.

**[Section 3: Technical Impact Assessment]{.underline}**

**[Affected Systems]{.underline}**

The LockStar 3.0 ransomware variant encrypted 347 of approximately 2,100 networked endpoints within the Vantage corporate IT environment. The affected systems are concentrated in the enterprise resource planning (\"ERP\") domain. Specifically, the following ERP modules were rendered unavailable as a result of the encryption:

> • **Finance Module** --- Supporting accounts payable, accounts receivable, general ledger, and financial reporting functions.
>
> • **Procurement Module** --- Supporting purchase order creation, vendor management, and supply chain transaction processing.
>
> • **Order Management Module** --- Supporting customer order entry, fulfillment tracking, and invoicing functions.

These three modules collectively support the Company\'s core transactional and financial operations. The loss of availability of these modules required the implementation of manual workaround processes, which have been in place since November 18, 2024.

**[Unaffected Systems]{.underline}**

Manufacturing operations and operational technology (\"OT\") / SCADA systems were not affected by the incident. These systems operate on an air-gapped network architecture that is physically and logically isolated from the corporate IT environment. This architectural control effectively prevented the ransomware from propagating to the manufacturing environment. Additionally, the Company\'s email platform, human resources information system, and customer-facing web portal were not compromised in this incident.

**[Attack Vector]{.underline}**

The initial access vector has been confirmed as a compromised VPN credential belonging to a third-party maintenance contractor. Thorngate\'s preliminary root cause analysis has identified that the compromised credential was not protected by multi-factor authentication (\"MFA\"). The absence of MFA on the third-party VPN access pathway permitted the threat actor to authenticate using the compromised credential without requiring a secondary verification factor.

**[Data Exfiltration Summary]{.underline}**

Approximately 83 GB of data was exfiltrated from the Vantage network between approximately 1:30 AM and 2:15 AM EST on November 18, 2024. The exfiltration occurred prior to the deployment of the ransomware payload, consistent with a deliberate two-phase attack methodology. Based on Thorngate\'s interim report dated December 1, 2024, the exfiltrated data is believed to include:

> • Customer master records for approximately 4,200 corporate clients
>
> • Customer names and procurement contact details constituting personally identifiable information (\"PII\")
>
> • Contract pricing information and terms
>
> • Banking information for ACH payments, including routing numbers and account numbers

**[Endpoint Restoration Status]{.underline}**

As of December 5, 2024, 289 of the 347 affected endpoints have been successfully restored from backups. The remaining 58 endpoints (347 − 289 = 58) could not be restored from standard backup images due to configuration complexities and require manual rebuild procedures. The estimated completion date for full endpoint restoration is December 18, 2024.

Thorngate Forensic Solutions\' full preliminary findings report is due December 20, 2024, in accordance with the 60-day timeline established in the November 19, 2024 engagement letter.

**[Section 4: Financial Impact Estimate]{.underline}**

The following financial impact estimates are preliminary and are based on costs incurred and projected through the date of this report. These figures are subject to revision as the investigation continues and as any customer claims, regulatory actions, or other third-party costs materialize. Final costs may be materially higher than the estimates presented below.

**[Cost Summary]{.underline}**

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Category**                                         **Estimated Cost**
  ---------------------------------------------------- -----------------------------------
  Incident Response Costs                              \$1,400,000

  Business Disruption Costs                            \$2,800,000

  Customer Notification Preparation Costs              \$300,000

  **Total Estimated Costs (as of December 5, 2024)**   **\$4,500,000**
  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Incident Response Costs --- \$1.4 million.** This category includes Thorngate Forensic Solutions\' engagement fees, emergency IT contractor labor for endpoint restoration and manual rebuilds, replacement hardware for endpoints that could not be recovered, and overtime for internal IT Security and IT Infrastructure staff since November 18, 2024.

**Business Disruption Costs --- \$2.8 million.** This category includes the estimated cost of delayed order processing resulting from the unavailability of the ERP finance, procurement, and order management modules; overtime labor costs for staff performing manual workaround processes; and estimated lost productivity across affected business units during the remediation period.

**Customer Notification Preparation Costs --- \$0.3 million.** This category includes the engagement of a third-party notification vendor, call center setup and staffing, and procurement of credit monitoring services for affected individuals. These services have been procured but have not yet been deployed pending finalization of the notification scope.

**Total Estimated Costs --- \$4.5 million** (\$1.4M + \$2.8M + \$0.3M = \$4.5M).

**[Ransom Demand]{.underline}**

The threat actor demanded 45 Bitcoin, which at the November 18, 2024 exchange rate of approximately \$45,100 per Bitcoin equates to approximately \$2,029,500 (calculated as 45 × \$45,100 = \$2,029,500). Vantage has elected not to pay the ransom. This amount is not included in the cost estimates above.

**[Insurance Coverage]{.underline}**

The Company maintains a cyber insurance policy with Ridgeline Insurance Group under Policy No. CYB-2024-08871. The key policy terms relevant to this incident are as follows:

> • **Aggregate Limit:** \$15,000,000
>
> • **Self-Insured Retention (SIR):** \$2,500,000

The total estimated costs of \$4.5 million exceed the SIR of \$2.5 million by \$2.0 million (\$4.5M − \$2.5M = \$2.0M), meaning the policy coverage layer has been triggered and Vantage would be eligible to recover up to \$2.0 million from Ridgeline under current cost estimates, subject to policy terms and conditions.

Ridgeline was notified at approximately 73 hours post-discovery. The policy requires notice within 72 hours. Ridgeline has reserved rights on the timeliness of notice but has not issued a coverage denial. The potential recovery of \$2.0 million above the SIR may be affected if Ridgeline asserts the late notice as grounds for denial or coverage limitation.

**[Financial Context]{.underline}**

FY2024 projected EBITDA is approximately \$310 million. The estimated \$4.5 million in incident costs represents approximately 1.45% of projected EBITDA (\$4.5M ÷ \$310M = 1.45%). These figures do not account for potential customer claims, regulatory penalties, litigation costs, or reputational impact, which could increase the total financial exposure.

**[Section 5: External Notifications and Communications Status]{.underline}**

This section provides a comprehensive summary of all external notifications and communications related to Incident INC-2024-0047, organized by stakeholder category.

**[5.1 Law Enforcement]{.underline}**

The FBI Internet Crime Complaint Center (\"IC3\") was contacted on November 20, 2024, to report the cybersecurity incident. The FBI acknowledged receipt of the report. As of the date of this report, there is no active FBI investigation that has been communicated to Vantage, and the Company continues to cooperate with law enforcement as requested. No request has been received from the U.S. Department of Justice or any law enforcement agency to delay public disclosure of this incident.

**[5.2 Cyber Insurance]{.underline}**

Ridgeline Insurance Group was notified of the cyber event on November 21, 2024, under Policy No. CYB-2024-08871. Notification was completed approximately 73 hours after the initial detection of the incident. As noted in Section 4, the policy requires notification within 72 hours of discovery. Ridgeline has assigned a claims adjuster to the matter. Ridgeline has reserved rights regarding the timeliness of notice but has not denied coverage at this time.

**[5.3 Customer Notifications]{.underline}**

No customer notifications have been sent as of December 5, 2024. Thorngate\'s December 1, 2024 interim report confirmed with high confidence that customer banking data, including ACH routing numbers and account numbers, was among the exfiltrated files. Affected records include approximately 4,200 corporate client accounts.

Notable affected customers include Harmon Defense Solutions, Crestfield Aerospace, Nexagen Manufacturing, and others among Vantage\'s largest commercial accounts. The Company\'s top 10 customers represent 38% of total revenue (\$1.87 billion × 38% = \$710.6 million). At least 6 of the top 10 customers are believed to have records in the exfiltrated dataset based on Thorngate\'s preliminary analysis.

Customer notification planning is in progress. The Legal department is coordinating with Thorngate to finalize the scope of affected records. Notification letters and call center scripts are being prepared (estimated \$0.3 million in preparation costs included in the financial impact estimate above). No target date for customer notification has been established.

**[5.4 Regulatory Filings]{.underline}**

**Securities and Exchange Commission.** No Form 8-K or other filing has been made with the Securities and Exchange Commission regarding this incident. No formal materiality determination has been conducted.

**State Regulators.** No notifications have been filed with any state attorney general or state data protection authority regarding the incident.

**[5.5 Board of Directors / Audit Committee]{.underline}**

Dr. Helen Ostrowski, Chair of the Audit Committee of the Board of Directors, received an email summary from General Counsel Priya Raghavan on December 3, 2024 (Day 15 post-detection). This email represented the first Board-level communication regarding the cybersecurity incident. The next regularly scheduled meeting of the Audit Committee is January 22, 2025. No special meeting of the Audit Committee has been called.

**[5.6 External Counsel]{.underline}**

Hollister Marsh LLP was engaged by General Counsel Priya Raghavan on November 20, 2024. The lead partner assigned to the matter is Jonathan Beckett. Hollister Marsh is providing securities law counsel regarding the Company\'s disclosure obligations in connection with this incident.

**[5.7 Public / Media]{.underline}**

No press release or other public statement regarding this incident has been issued. No media inquiries have been received as of the date of this report.

**[5.8 Independent Auditor]{.underline}**

Greystone & Associates LLP, the Company\'s independent auditor, has not been notified of the incident. The FY2024 audit cycle has not yet commenced. The Company\'s fiscal year ends December 31, 2024.

**[5.9 Investment Bank / M&A Advisors]{.underline}**

Vantage is currently in active negotiations for the acquisition of Kessler Precision Systems GmbH (estimated transaction value: \$425 million). Canfield Cromdale Consulting & Co. is advising Vantage on the transaction. As of this report date, neither Kessler Precision Systems GmbH nor Canfield Cromdale Consulting & Co. has been notified of the cybersecurity incident. The potential impact of this incident on the pending transaction has not been assessed.

**[Section 6: Remediation Status and Next Steps]{.underline}**

The following summarizes the current remediation status and planned next steps for each active workstream.

**Endpoint Restoration.** As of December 5, 2024, 289 of 347 affected endpoints have been restored from backup images. The remaining 58 endpoints require manual rebuild procedures due to configuration complexities that prevented standard backup restoration. The manual rebuild process is labor-intensive and is being executed by a combination of internal IT Infrastructure staff and emergency IT contractors. The estimated completion date for full endpoint restoration is December 18, 2024. Upon completion, all affected ERP modules --- finance, procurement, and order management --- are expected to be fully operational without the need for manual workaround processes.

**VPN Access Controls.** All third-party VPN access remains suspended as of the date of this report. The IT Security team is implementing mandatory multi-factor authentication (\"MFA\") for all VPN connections, including both internal and third-party users. Third-party VPN access will not be restored until MFA implementation is complete and validated. An estimated completion date for MFA deployment has not yet been established.

**Credential Reset.** All user credentials associated with the compromised contractor account, as well as all accounts identified as having similar access patterns or privilege levels, have been reset. Affected users were required to authenticate through the Company\'s identity management platform with new credentials.

**Network Monitoring.** Enhanced network monitoring and active threat hunting are ongoing. The SOC has implemented additional detection rules and behavioral analytics tailored to the LockStar 3.0 indicator of compromise profile. As of the date of this report, no evidence of continued unauthorized access has been identified since containment actions were implemented on November 18, 2024.

**Thorngate Forensic Investigation.** Thorngate Forensic Solutions\' full preliminary findings report is due December 20, 2024, in accordance with the 60-day engagement timeline commencing from the November 19, 2024 retention date. A final report is due within 60 days of the preliminary report, or approximately January 17, 2025.

**Customer Notification.** Notification planning is in progress as described in Section 5.3 above. No target date has been established pending finalization of the affected records scope.

**Incident Documentation.** All incident-related activities, communications, decisions, and remediation actions are being documented in ServiceNow under Incident Ticket INC-2024-0047. A post-incident review will be conducted in accordance with Phase 5 of the CIRP following the completion of all active remediation workstreams.

**[Section 7: Open Items and Risk Factors]{.underline}**

The following open items and risk factors have been identified as of the date of this report. These items require continued monitoring and, in several cases, additional action or decision-making by the appropriate stakeholders.

1\. **Forensic Investigation Scope.** The Thorngate forensic investigation is ongoing. The full scope of the data exfiltration has not yet been conclusively confirmed. Thorngate\'s interim report identified approximately 4,200 affected customer records with high confidence, but additional affected records may be identified as the analysis continues. The full preliminary findings report is due December 20, 2024.

2\. **Remaining Endpoint Restoration.** Fifty-eight (58) endpoints remain unrestored and require manual rebuild procedures. The manual rebuild process is labor-intensive and is dependent upon the continued availability of emergency IT contractor resources. The estimated completion date of December 18, 2024 is subject to revision based on resource availability and technical complexity.

3\. **Customer Notification Timing and Scope.** Customer notification timing and scope have not been finalized. The Legal department is awaiting Thorngate\'s final scope analysis before initiating notifications. No determination has been made regarding customer contractual obligations related to the incident.

4\. **Insurance Coverage Uncertainty.** Ridgeline Insurance Group has reserved rights on the timeliness of notice. Potential recovery of \$2.0 million (\$4.5M total estimated costs − \$2.5M SIR) may be at risk if Ridgeline determines that the approximately one-hour delay in notification (73 hours versus the 72-hour contractual requirement) constitutes grounds for coverage denial or limitation.

5\. **SEC Disclosure.** No formal materiality determination has been conducted for SEC disclosure purposes. Legal department and outside counsel (Hollister Marsh LLP) are evaluating disclosure obligations.

6\. Pending Acquisition Impact. The potential impact of this cybersecurity incident on the pending acquisition of Kessler Precision Systems GmbH (estimated transaction value: \$425 million) has not been evaluated. Neither the transaction counterparty nor the Company\'s financial advisor (Canfield Cromdale Consulting & Co.) has been notified of the incident.

7\. **State Regulatory Notifications.** No determinations have been made regarding notification obligations under applicable state data breach notification statutes.

**[Appendix A: Incident Response Team Activation Record]{.underline}**

The following Incident Response Team members were activated on November 18, 2024, at 5:00 AM EST, pursuant to CIRP Section 3.2 (Incident Response Team Roster), under Tier 3 classification:

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Role**                    **Name / Designation**                           **Activation Time**
  --------------------------- ------------------------------------------------ -----------------------

  IT Infrastructure Lead      Per CIRP Section 3.2 designation                 5:00 AM EST

  HR Representative           Per CIRP Section 3.2 designation                 5:00 AM EST
  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

IRT composition per CIRP Section 3.2 (Incident Response Team Roster). All activated members reported to the designated incident war room by 5:30 AM EST on November 18, 2024.

**[Appendix B: Key Contact Information]{.underline}**

**CYBER LIABILITY INSURANCE POLICY** **SUMMARY OF MATERIAL TERMS**

**Policy Number:** CYB-2024-08871

**Insurer:** Ridgeline Insurance Group

**Named Insured:** Vantage Industrial Technologies, Inc., a Delaware corporation 4200 Commerce Park Drive, Suite 800 Columbus, Ohio 43219

This summary is provided for internal reference purposes only and does not constitute the policy itself. In the event of any conflict between this summary and the Policy, the Policy governs. All capitalized terms not otherwise defined herein have the meanings ascribed to them in the Policy.

**[Section 1: Policy Information and Term]{.underline}**

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Field**                           **Detail**
  ----------------------------------- -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Policy Number**                   CYB-2024-08871

  **Insurer**                         Ridgeline Insurance Group

  **Named Insured**                   Vantage Industrial Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ: VTIQ)

  **Policy Period**                   January 1, 2024 through December 31, 2024, 12:01 AM Eastern Standard Time

  **Policy Type**                     Claims-Made and Reported Cyber Liability Insurance Policy

  **Territory**                       Worldwide, for Claims brought in the United States, its territories, or Canada. Coverage for Claims brought in any other jurisdiction is subject to the prior written consent of the Insurer.

  **Broker of Record**                Per broker agreement on file
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[Section 2: Definitions]{.underline}**

The following key terms are defined in the Policy and used throughout this summary:

**\"Cyber Event\"** means any unauthorized access to, unauthorized use of, or malicious disruption of the Insured\'s Computer Systems, including but not limited to ransomware, malware, phishing, denial-of-service attacks, and data exfiltration. The event must originate from an external threat actor or a rogue insider acting without the authorization of the Named Insured\'s management.

**\"Computer Systems\"** means hardware, software, firmware, networks, and data storage systems owned, operated, or leased by the Named Insured, including cloud-hosted environments managed by third-party service providers under written contract with the Named Insured.

**\"Claim\"** means a written demand for monetary damages or non-monetary relief, a civil proceeding, regulatory investigation or inquiry, or arbitration proceeding arising out of a Cyber Event.

**\"Discovery\"** means the point in time at which the Named Insured\'s Chief Information Security Officer, Chief Information Officer, Chief Technology Officer, General Counsel, Chief Financial Officer, or any Vice President-level or above officer first becomes aware of facts that would cause a reasonable person in such position to believe that a Cyber Event has occurred or is occurring. Discovery by any one such individual constitutes Discovery by the Named Insured for all purposes under the Policy, including the commencement of all applicable notice periods.

**\"First-Party Loss\"** means costs and expenses incurred directly by the Named Insured as a result of a Cyber Event, including Incident Response Costs, Business Interruption Loss, Data Restoration Costs, and Cyber Extortion Loss.

**\"Third-Party Loss\"** means damages, settlements, judgments, and Defense Costs the Named Insured becomes legally obligated to pay as a result of a Claim arising from a Cyber Event.

**\"Incident Response Costs\"** means reasonable and necessary fees and expenses for forensic investigation, breach notification, credit monitoring, public relations, and legal counsel incurred with the prior written consent of the Insurer or, in urgent circumstances, ratified by the Insurer within ten (10) business days.

**\"Business Interruption Loss\"** means net income loss and extra expense incurred during the Restoration Period directly resulting from a material interruption or degradation of the Named Insured\'s Computer Systems caused by a Cyber Event, subject to a waiting period of eight (8) hours.

**\"Restoration Period\"** means the period beginning at the time of the Cyber Event and ending on the earlier of (a) the date Computer Systems are restored to substantially the same operational condition as existed immediately prior to the Cyber Event, or (b) one hundred eighty (180) days after the Cyber Event.

**[Section 3: Coverage --- Insuring Agreements]{.underline}**

The Policy provides coverage under two principal insuring agreements: First-Party Coverage (Insuring Agreement A) and Third-Party Coverage (Insuring Agreement B).

**3.1 Insuring Agreement A --- First-Party Coverage**

**A.1 --- Incident Response Costs.** The Insurer will reimburse the Named Insured for reasonable and necessary costs incurred in response to a Cyber Event, including fees for forensic investigation, legal advisory services, breach notification and call center services, credit and identity monitoring for affected individuals, and crisis communications and public relations. Forensic investigation vendors must be selected from the Insurer\'s Pre-Approved Vendor Panel (see Section 8 and Exhibit A to the Policy) or approved in writing by the Insurer prior to engagement. Thorngate Forensic Solutions, Inc. is included on the Pre-Approved Vendor Panel.

**A.2 --- Business Interruption Loss.** The Insurer will reimburse the Named Insured for net income loss and extra expense resulting from material interruption of Computer Systems caused by a Cyber Event, subject to the eight (8) hour waiting period and the Restoration Period cap of one hundred eighty (180) days.

**A.3 --- Data Restoration Costs.** The Insurer will reimburse the Named Insured for reasonable costs to restore, recreate, or recollect electronic data that is corrupted, destroyed, or encrypted as a direct result of a Cyber Event, including costs of hardware replacement where hardware is rendered permanently inoperable by the Cyber Event.

**A.4 --- Cyber Extortion Loss.** The Insurer will reimburse ransom payments made in cryptocurrency or fiat currency in response to a credible extortion threat, plus reasonable costs of negotiation, provided that: (i) the Named Insured obtains the Insurer\'s prior written consent before making any payment; and (ii) all payments comply with applicable laws, including OFAC sanctions requirements. No coverage is available for ransom payments that violate OFAC sanctions (see Endorsement No. 4).

**3.2 Insuring Agreement B --- Third-Party Coverage**

**B.1 --- Network Security Liability.** Covers damages and Defense Costs arising from Claims by third parties alleging failure to prevent unauthorized access to Computer Systems, transmission of malicious code, or denial of service originating from the Named Insured\'s Computer Systems.

**B.2 --- Privacy Liability.** Covers damages and Defense Costs arising from Claims alleging failure to protect confidential information---including personal information and confidential commercial information of third parties---in the Named Insured\'s care, custody, or control. This coverage expressly includes Claims arising from data exfiltration events.

**B.3 --- Regulatory Defense and Penalties.** Covers Defense Costs and, where insurable by law, penalties and fines arising from regulatory proceedings brought by any federal, state, or foreign regulatory authority in connection with a Cyber Event, including proceedings under state data breach notification statutes.

**B.4 --- Media Liability.** Covers Claims arising from the Named Insured\'s publication of content in electronic media, including allegations of defamation, invasion of privacy, or copyright infringement in electronic publications.

**[Section 4: Limits of Liability and Self-Insured Retention]{.underline}**

  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Coverage**                                                                  **Limit**
  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- --------------------------------------
  **Aggregate Limit of Liability** (all Insuring Agreements A and B combined)   \$15,000,000

  **Per-Occurrence Limit**                                                      \$15,000,000 (shared with aggregate)

  **Self-Insured Retention (SIR)** per Cyber Event                              \$2,500,000
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Sub-Limits** (within the aggregate, per Cyber Event):

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Coverage Component**                     **Sub-Limit**
  ------------------------------------------ -----------------------------------
  Cyber Extortion Loss                       \$5,000,000

  Regulatory Defense and Penalties           \$7,500,000

  Crisis Communications / Public Relations   \$1,000,000

  PCI-DSS Assessment Coverage                \$3,000,000
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Named Insured is responsible for all First-Party Loss and Third-Party Loss up to the SIR before the Insurer\'s payment obligation attaches. The SIR applies separately to each Cyber Event; however, interrelated Cyber Events---meaning events arising from the same or related facts, circumstances, situations, transactions, or threat actors---shall be treated as a single Cyber Event for purposes of the SIR and the limits of liability.

**Defense Costs** erode the applicable limit of liability. The Policy provides a duty to reimburse Defense Costs, not a duty to defend.

**[Section 5: Notice and Cooperation Provisions]{.underline}**

**5.1 Notice of Cyber Event**

The Named Insured shall provide written notice to the Insurer of any Cyber Event as soon as practicable, but **in no event later than seventy-two (72) hours after Discovery** of such Cyber Event.

Notice shall be directed to:

> Ridgeline Insurance Group Cyber Claims Division 1500 Lakefront Boulevard, Suite 400 Hartford, Connecticut 06103
>
> Email: cyberclaims@ridgelineinsurance.com
>
> Telephone: (860) 555-0147

Notice must include: (a) the Named Insured\'s policy number (CYB-2024-08871); (b) a description of the Cyber Event including date and time of Discovery; (c) the nature and scope of Computer Systems affected; (d) a preliminary description of any data compromised or exfiltrated; (e) identification of any law enforcement agencies contacted; and (f) the identity of any forensic investigation firms retained or proposed to be retained.

The seventy-two (72) hour notice period commences at the time of Discovery, as that term is defined in Section 2 of this summary and in the Policy. Discovery occurs when any qualifying officer (CISO, CIO, CTO, General Counsel, CFO, or any VP-level or above officer) first becomes aware of facts that would cause a reasonable person to believe a Cyber Event has occurred or is occurring. Awareness by any one qualifying officer constitutes Discovery by the Named Insured.

**5.2 Notice of Claim**

The Named Insured shall provide written notice to the Insurer of any Claim as soon as practicable, but in no event later than thirty (30) days after the Named Insured first receives such Claim. For Claims-made coverage, the Claim must be first made against the Named Insured and reported to the Insurer during the Policy Period or any applicable Extended Reporting Period.

**5.3 Consequences of Late Notice**

Failure to provide timely notice as required under Sections 5.1 or 5.2 shall not void coverage unless the Insurer demonstrates that it was **materially prejudiced** by such late notice; provided, however, that the Insurer reserves all rights and defenses available under applicable law with respect to late notice, including the right to deny coverage in whole or in part if the late notice materially prejudices the Insurer\'s ability to investigate the Cyber Event, mitigate losses, or exercise its subrogation rights.

In jurisdictions where a \"notice-prejudice\" rule applies as a matter of law, the Insurer\'s rights with respect to late notice shall be governed by applicable law. In jurisdictions where strict compliance with notice provisions is required as a condition precedent to coverage, failure to provide timely notice shall be a ground for denial of coverage. The Insurer\'s acceptance or investigation of a late-noticed Cyber Event or Claim shall not constitute a waiver of the Insurer\'s rights under this provision or under applicable law.

**5.4 Cooperation.** The Named Insured shall cooperate fully with the Insurer in the investigation, defense, and settlement of any Claim or Cyber Event, including: (a) providing all documentation, records, and communications related to the Cyber Event; (b) making employees, officers, and agents available for interviews and testimony; (c) not admitting liability or making voluntary payments without the Insurer\'s prior written consent; and (d) taking all reasonable steps to mitigate losses. The Named Insured shall not settle any Claim or agree to pay any ransom without the Insurer\'s prior written consent, which shall not be unreasonably withheld.

**5.5 Reservation of Rights**

The Insurer may, at any time, issue a reservation of rights letter without waiving any rights or defenses under the Policy. Issuance of a reservation of rights letter does not constitute a denial of coverage, nor does it create an obligation on the part of the Insurer to provide coverage.

**[Section 6: Exclusions]{.underline}**

The Policy contains the following exclusions, among others. Coverage does not apply to:

**(a) Prior Knowledge.** Any Cyber Event or circumstances that could give rise to a Claim known to any insured person prior to the Policy Period inception date of January 1, 2024.

**(b) Intentional or Dishonest Acts.** Loss arising from any intentional, dishonest, fraudulent, or criminal act of the Named Insured\'s directors, officers, or employees, unless attributable to a rogue employee acting without the knowledge or consent of management.

**(c) War and Terrorism.** Loss arising from acts of war (declared or undeclared), military action, or terrorism, except for cyberterrorism events defined as attacks by non-state actors without the direction or substantial support of a nation-state. State-sponsored cyberattacks are excluded under the hostile nation-state cyber operations sub-exclusion.

**(d) Infrastructure Failure.** Loss arising from failure of electrical grids, telecommunications networks, or internet infrastructure not under the Named Insured\'s control, unless such failure is caused by a targeted cyberattack directed at the Named Insured.

**(e) Bodily Injury and Property Damage.** Bodily injury or tangible property damage, except for physical damage to the Named Insured\'s Computer Systems directly caused by a Cyber Event.

**(f) Patent and Trade Secret Infringement.** Claims alleging patent infringement or misappropriation of trade secrets.

**(g) Contractual Liability.** Loss arising from breach of contract, unless the Named Insured would have been liable in the absence of such contract. Liability arising solely from breach of contractual notification or indemnification clauses---where no independent tort liability exists---may be excluded under this provision.

**(h) Unencrypted Portable Devices.** Loss arising from theft or loss of unencrypted portable devices or media, unless the Named Insured demonstrates that reasonable encryption controls were in place.

**(i) Failure to Maintain Minimum Security Standards.** Loss arising from the Named Insured\'s failure to maintain security controls substantially consistent with those represented in the Policy Application, provided that such failure materially contributed to the Cyber Event.

**[Section 7: Conditions]{.underline}**

**7.1 Duties After a Cyber Event**

In addition to the notice requirements set forth in Section 5, the Named Insured must: (a) take all reasonable and necessary steps to contain the Cyber Event and prevent further unauthorized access; (b) preserve all evidence, logs, and forensic data related to the Cyber Event; (c) not destroy, alter, or dispose of any Computer Systems, hardware, or media affected by the Cyber Event without the Insurer\'s written consent; and (d) comply with all applicable federal, state, and local laws regarding data breach notification.

**7.2 Consent to Incur Costs**

First-Party Losses exceeding \$50,000 individually or \$150,000 in the aggregate require the prior written consent of the Insurer. In emergency circumstances where delay would materially increase the loss, the Named Insured may incur costs without prior consent, provided that: (a) the Named Insured notifies the Insurer within forty-eight (48) hours of incurring such costs; and (b) the costs are reasonable and necessary.

Forensic investigation firms must be selected from the Insurer\'s Pre-Approved Vendor Panel (Exhibit A to the Policy) unless the Insurer provides written consent to retain a different vendor. **Thorngate Forensic Solutions, Inc.** is listed on the Pre-Approved Vendor Panel.

**7.3 Subrogation.** The Insurer shall be subrogated to the Named Insured\'s rights of recovery against any third party responsible for or contributing to the Cyber Event, to the extent of any payment made under the Policy. The Named Insured shall not take any action that would prejudice the Insurer\'s subrogation rights.

**7.4 Other Insurance**

This Policy is excess over any other valid and collectible insurance available to the Named Insured, except for insurance specifically written to be excess of this Policy.

**7.5 Choice of Law and Dispute Resolution**

The Policy shall be governed by the laws of the State of Ohio, the Named Insured\'s principal place of business. Any dispute arising under or relating to the Policy shall be resolved by binding arbitration in Columbus, Ohio, conducted under the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

**[Section 8: Pre-Approved Vendor Panel (Summary)]{.underline}**

Exhibit A to the Policy identifies the following pre-approved vendors for incident response services:

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Category**                              **Approved Vendor(s)**
  ----------------------------------------- -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Digital Forensics**                     Thorngate Forensic Solutions, Inc.; Redcliff Cyber Analytics, LLC; Pemberton Digital Investigations, Inc.

  **Breach Notification / Call Center**     Arroyo Notification Services, Inc.

  **Legal --- Incident Response Counsel**   Hollister Marsh LLP; Clarendon Pike & Somers LLP

  **Credit Monitoring**                     Sentinel Identity Protection Corp.

  **Crisis Communications / PR**            Whitfield Strategic Communications, LLC
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Retention of vendors not on the Pre-Approved Vendor Panel requires the Insurer\'s prior written consent (see Section 7.2).

**[Section 9: Policy Application Warranty]{.underline}**

The Named Insured\'s Policy Application dated October 15, 2023 (for the January 1, 2024 -- December 31, 2024 Policy Period) is incorporated into the Policy by reference. In the application, the Named Insured warranted that: (a) it maintains a written cybersecurity incident response plan; (b) it employs multi-factor authentication for all remote access to Computer Systems; (c) it maintains encrypted backups stored offline or in a segregated environment; (d) it has endpoint detection and response (EDR) deployed across all networked endpoints; and (e) it conducts employee cybersecurity awareness training at least annually. The accuracy of these representations is a condition of coverage. Material misrepresentations in the Policy Application may void coverage ab initio.

**[Section 10: Endorsements Applicable to This Policy]{.underline}**

The following endorsements are attached to and form part of the Policy:

**Endorsement No. 1 --- Ransomware Coverage Enhancement.** Extends Cyber Extortion Loss coverage under Insuring Agreement A.4 to include costs of cryptocurrency procurement, conversion, and transaction fees incurred in connection with an authorized ransom payment.

**Endorsement No. 2 --- Dependent Business Interruption.** Extends Business Interruption Loss coverage under Insuring Agreement A.2 to include losses caused by Cyber Events affecting the Named Insured\'s critical third-party service providers, subject to a \$3,000,000 sub-limit per Cyber Event.

**Endorsement No. 3 --- Social Engineering Fraud.** Provides limited coverage for direct financial loss resulting from the fraudulent transfer of funds induced by social engineering, subject to a \$500,000 sub-limit per Cyber Event.

**Endorsement No. 4 --- OFAC Compliance Carve-Back.** Clarifies that no coverage is provided under any Insuring Agreement for ransom payments or other transfers that violate OFAC sanctions regulations or any other applicable sanctions laws.

**Endorsement No. 5 --- Regulatory Event Coverage Expansion.** Broadens Insuring Agreement B.3 to include investigations and proceedings by GDPR supervisory authorities and EU member state data protection authorities, subject to applicable law and the Regulatory Defense and Penalties sub-limit.

**DISCLAIMER:** This summary was prepared by the Office of the General Counsel of Vantage Industrial Technologies, Inc. for internal reference only. It is not a substitute for Policy No. CYB-2024-08871 as issued by Ridgeline Insurance Group and should not be relied upon as a definitive statement of coverage. All coverage determinations are subject to the terms, conditions, and exclusions of the Policy. For questions regarding coverage, contact Priya Raghavan, General Counsel, or Daniel Ito, Associate General Counsel.

Prepared by: Office of the General Counsel Vantage Industrial Technologies, Inc.
